title: UTC Technical Note
subtitle: Draft note on compatibility, spatial composition, and next steps
summary: A formal draft note stating that current official Unicode SignWriting is not sufficient for compatible Sutton SignWriting production use.
synopsis: This draft UTC note states the current technical position in a formal register. It defines the problem, explains why current official Unicode SignWriting is not compatible with the Sutton SignWriting production ecosystem, identifies spatial composition and collation as unresolved issues, and requests renewed technical review rather than automatic migration pressure.

slides.title: Current UTC-Facing Position
slides.summary: The current official Unicode SignWriting encoding is not sufficient for compatible production use within the Sutton SignWriting ecosystem.
slides.image_alt: Placeholder image for UTC technical note.
bullets: A renewed technical path should begin from the real ecosystem
slides.speech: The current UTC-facing position is disciplined and technical. The official Unicode SignWriting encoding is not sufficient for compatible production use within the Sutton SignWriting ecosystem. The core issues include compatibility, spatial composition, collation, and migration. Any renewed Unicode discussion should begin from the reality of the existing production ecosystem rather than from the assumption that the symbol block alone solved the writing-system problem.
slides.notes: This should stay formal and measured.
